summary: Verified the Services page image bug fix. All 5 service cards (car, motorcycle, computer, phone, heavy) now load their images correctly in both EN and TET modes — heavy card no longer shows the broken-image icon with 'heavy' alt text. Regression items (titles, descriptions, 'View Details / Haree Detallu' links) all render correctly. IMPORTANT: On the very first test run the browser was still receiving the OLD broken URL (photo-1581093458791) even though the source file already contained the new URL — a supervisor restart of the frontend was required to pick up the change (webpack HMR appeared to have stalled). After restart, the new URL (photo-1518709268805) loads successfully with naturalWidth=900.

issues: The new image (photo-1518709268805-4e9042af9f23) technically loads correctly, but its content is a stone castle/fortress gate rather than heavy equipment (forklift/tractor/loader/heavy-duty truck). The image is inconsistent with the card title 'Heavy Equipment Guarantee / Garantia Pezadu' and the description mentioning forklift/tractor/loader. Recommend swapping to a properly themed heavy-equipment Unsplash photo. The same URL is also used in /app/frontend/src/pages/public/Home.js — both should be updated together for consistency.

Minor: `` uses the internal key (e.g. 'heavy') as alt text — this is why the user saw the word 'heavy' when the image failed. Consider using the localized title (titleEn/titleTet) as alt text for better accessibility and to avoid leaking internal identifiers when an image fails.

action_items: Replace the heavy-equipment image URL with a genuinely heavy-equipment themed Unsplash photo (current URL loads a castle image). Update both /app/frontend/src/pages/public/Services.js (line 45) and /app/frontend/src/pages/public/Home.js.

Optional accessibility improvement: change `alt={s.key}` to `alt={lang === 'tet' ? s.titleTet : s.titleEn}` in Services.js line 74 so failed-image fallback text is user-friendly rather than an internal key.
No further action required for the broken-image bug itself — it is resolved.
critical_code_review_comments: Services.js line 74 uses `alt={s.key}` — leaks internal identifier; prefer localized title for a11y.
Same image URL constant is duplicated between Services.js and Home.js — consider extracting shared SERVICES data into a common module to avoid drift.
External Unsplash CDN dependence: any of these image URLs could 404 in future. Consider hosting critical marketing imagery locally or adding an onError fallback () to swap to a placeholder.

success_rate.frontend: 100% for image-loading; heavy image content mismatch flagged as design issue

context_for_next_testing_agent: The broken-image bug on /services heavy card is resolved. All 5 images load (naturalWidth > 0). The heavy-card image, however, is visually a stone castle rather than heavy equipment — this is a content/theme issue for the main agent to address if desired. Note: a supervisor restart of the frontend was needed for the URL swap to take effect; webpack HMR did not pick it up on its own initially.
rca of the issue: Original bug: photo-1581093458791-9d15482442f6 returned 404 from Unsplash, so the failed to render and browsers displayed the alt attribute text ('heavy'). Fix: swap to photo-1518709268805-4e9042af9f23 (returns 200, naturalWidth 900). Verification: Playwright checks img.naturalWidth > 0 for all 5 cards in both EN and TET — passes after frontend restart.
